input.plan:
# Reelink Final Completion Plan

## Non-Negotiable Authority

- Treat `transcript-combined.md` as the highest-order source of product truth. Later user corrections in that transcript override earlier generated architecture or hackathon shortcuts.
- Treat current `reelink/` code as implementation-status evidence only, not product intent.
- Treat `completion-auditreview.md` as the most aligned audit because it uses transcript-first authority.
- Treat `REELINK_COMPLETION_AUDIT.md` and `/Users/harsha/.cursor/plans/reelink_completion_fb9c0886.plan.md` as stale operational inputs where they agree with the transcript.
- Update all plan/audit/status docs that currently put the old completion plan above the transcript.

## Final Product Target

Reelink is an open-source video/state primitive for coding agents. The product is not merely an AI bug reporter or an MCP wrapper.

Layer 0 must support existing video import: a user provides `.mov`, `.mp4`, or `.webm`, Reelink sends raw video to Qwen/OpenRouter when supported, returns public `findings`, and persists a queryable recording folder.

Layer 1 must support full browser recording: Reelink opens a browser, captures video plus DOM/runtime/network/console/trace/React/source evidence, persists timestamp-aligned artifacts, and lets agents query frames, DOM, components, and findings by timestamp.

Layer 2 must support agent self-recording: `reelink_run` records the agent’s browser task, preserves partial-failure evidence, returns `success`, recent observation fields, and durable eval evidence.

## Target Architecture

```mermaid
flowchart TD
  userAgent["Coding Agent"] --> reelinkMcp["Single Reelink MCP"]
  reelinkMcp --> analysis["Raw Video Analysis"]
  reelinkMcp --> browserGateway["Browser Gateway Tools"]
  reelinkMcp --> recording["Recording Lifecycle"]
  browserGateway --> childPlaywrightMcp["Managed Playwright MCP Child"]
  recording --> playwrightLibrary["Playwright Library"]
  playwrightLibrary --> chromium["Shared Chromium Session"]
  childPlaywrightMcp --> chromium
  recording --> reactGrab["Visible React-Grab Capture"]
  reactGrab --> artifacts["Timestamped Recording Package"]
  analysis --> artifacts
  artifacts --> retrieval["Frame DOM Component Query Eval"]
  retrieval --> userAgent
```

- The user registers exactly one MCP: `reelink`.
- Reelink may manage Playwright MCP internally as a child process attached to the same Chromium via CDP, but users must not register Playwright MCP separately.
- Playwright library remains the owner of recording, trace, video, and `addInitScript` order.
- `react-grab` is default, visible, and product-critical. The glow/click/annotate/source-path workflow must work, not just dependency resolution.
- AI SDK is the model abstraction. Do not use a direct OpenAI-client path as the primary model integration.

## Phase 1: Rewrite Authority And Status Artifacts

Files:
- [completion-auditreview.md](completion-auditreview.md)
- [REELINK_COMPLETION_AUDIT.md](REELINK_COMPLETION_AUDIT.md)
- `/Users/harsha/.cursor/plans/reelink_completion_fb9c0886.plan.md`
- [openspec/changes/build-reelink-mcp/tasks.md](openspec/changes/build-reelink-mcp/tasks.md)
- [openspec/changes/build-reelink-mcp/design.md](openspec/changes/build-reelink-mcp/design.md)

Work:
- Replace old authority ordering with transcript-first ordering.
- Stop calling `REELINK_COMPLETION_AUDIT.md` canonical.
- Rewrite `tasks.md` from binary checkboxes into explicit statuses: `planned`, `implemented`, `unit-tested`, `smoke-verified`, `external/manual`, `complete`.
- Separate “code exists” from “verified in a real app”.
- Mark `tasks.md` completion claims invalid until implementation, tests, smokes, docs, and OpenSpec validation pass.

Acceptance:
- No doc says the old completion plan outranks the transcript.
- `tasks.md` no longer says an area is pending at the top and complete later.

## Phase 2: Reconcile OpenSpec

Files:
- [openspec/changes/build-reelink-mcp/proposal.md](openspec/changes/build-reelink-mcp/proposal.md)
- [openspec/changes/build-reelink-mcp/design.md](openspec/changes/build-reelink-mcp/design.md)
- [openspec/changes/build-reelink-mcp/tasks.md](openspec/changes/build-reelink-mcp/tasks.md)
- [openspec/changes/build-reelink-mcp/specs/video-finding-analysis/spec.md](openspec/changes/build-reelink-mcp/specs/video-finding-analysis/spec.md)

- [openspec/changes/build-reelink-mcp/specs/react-fiber-source-map/spec.md](openspec/changes/build-reelink-mcp/specs/react-fiber-source-map/spec.md)
- [openspec/specs/react-fiber-source-map/spec.md](openspec/specs/react-fiber-source-map/spec.md)
- [openspec/changes/build-reelink-mcp/specs/timestamped-dom-timeline/spec.md](openspec/changes/build-reelink-mcp/specs/timestamped-dom-timeline/spec.md)
- [openspec/specs/timestamped-dom-timeline/spec.md](openspec/specs/timestamped-dom-timeline/spec.md)

- [openspec/changes/build-reelink-mcp/specs/eval-evidence-generation/spec.md](openspec/changes/build-reelink-mcp/specs/eval-evidence-generation/spec.md)
- [openspec/specs/eval-evidence-generation/spec.md](openspec/specs/eval-evidence-generation/spec.md)

Work:
- Normalize production install commands to `bunx -y reelink init`, `bunx -y reelink doctor`, and `bunx -y reelink mcp`, with `bun install -g reelink` as the global option.
- Scope `reelink-mcp`, source-path `bun run .../src/cli.ts mcp`, and `bun link` to local development only.
- Resolve active-spec vs change-spec sync state.
- Encode one query story: deterministic-first, optional AI SDK fallback only when explicitly enabled and configured, or remove fallback from v0.1 claims.
- Keep `findings` as the public Layer 0 contract. Define `work_items` only as internal/detail/compatibility if retained.
- Clarify eval evidence: durable evidence is required; generated deterministic tests are required only if actually implemented and run.
- Clarify `agentation-compatible markdown` means projection format only, no `agentation` dependency.

Acceptance:
- Active specs and change specs agree.
- OpenSpec no longer mixes `npx`, `bunx`, linked-local commands, and source-path commands as if they are the same product path.

## Phase 3: Public MCP Contract Cleanup

Files:
- [reelink/src/mcp/tools/analysis.ts](reelink/src/mcp/tools/analysis.ts)
- [reelink/src/analysis/pipeline.ts](reelink/src/analysis/pipeline.ts)
- [reelink/src/schemas/layer0.ts](reelink/src/schemas/layer0.ts)
- [reelink/src/mcp/tools/retrieval.ts](reelink/src/mcp/tools/retrieval.ts)
- [reelink/src/runtime-artifacts/retrieval.ts](reelink/src/runtime-artifacts/retrieval.ts)
- [reelink/src/query/index.ts](reelink/src/query/index.ts)
- [reelink/src/query/gpt-fallback.ts](reelink/src/query/gpt-fallback.ts)
- [reelink/tests/mcp-tool-surface.test.ts](reelink/tests/mcp-tool-surface.test.ts)
- [reelink/tests/analyze-orchestration.test.ts](reelink/tests/analyze-orchestration.test.ts)
- [reelink/tests/mcp-query-tools.test.ts](reelink/tests/mcp-query-tools.test.ts)
- [reelink/tests/deterministic-query.test.ts](reelink/tests/deterministic-query.test.ts)

Work:
- Make `findings` the canonical public output of `reelink_analyze`.
- Decide the final `work_items` policy:
  - Preferred: keep full `work_items` in stored analysis and retrieval detail, but document it as internal/detail compatibility rather than the public headline contract.
- Fix `reelink_get_finding` so the response shape does not say `finding_id` while returning only `work_item` without explanation.
- Expand `reelink_get_components` MCP `outputSchema` to match the richer runtime response, or trim runtime output to match the schema. Prefer expanding the public schema because the final product needs rich component/source evidence.
- Tighten `reelink_query` answer variants so clients can rely on stable response kinds.
- Rename query pattern names away from `*_findings` if the answer objects are work items, or consistently project them into findings.

# Reelink Master Completion Plan

## Scope And Authority

This plan supersedes the outdated `/Users/harsha/.cursor/plans/reelink_completion_fb9c0886.plan.md`. Do not execute or reconcile against that old plan except as historical context if a specific claim needs archaeology.

Authority order:

1. `transcript-combined.md`, with later user corrections overriding earlier generated architecture.
2. Current `reelink/` implementation as status evidence, not product intent.
3. Reconciled OpenSpec artifacts.
4. `completion-auditreview.md` as a useful transcript-first audit input.
5. `REELINK_COMPLETION_AUDIT.md` only as stale audit history where still accurate.

Execution principle:

- Prefer proving and tightening existing behavior over rewriting it.
- Before changing `reelink/src/mcp/tools/recording.ts`, `reelink/src/runtime-artifacts/retrieval.ts`, `reelink/src/recordings/store.ts`, or `reelink/src/agent-run/run.ts`, verify whether the current code already satisfies the transcript-defined end state.
- Only edit implementation where evidence shows a real gap.

## Final Product Target

Reelink is an open-source video/state primitive for coding agents.

Layer 0 imports existing video and returns structured findings. Raw video to Qwen/OpenRouter is primary where supported; frame extraction is for cached retrieval or fallback providers, not the product thesis.

Layer 1 records browser sessions into timestamp-aligned state packages: video, frames, DOM/runtime state, network, console, trace, react-grab annotation events, React/component/source evidence, manifest, and explicit missing-stream statuses.

Layer 2 records the agent’s own browser work through `reelink_run`, preserves evidence on partial failure, returns `success`, recent observations, and durable eval evidence.

```mermaid
flowchart TD
  agent["Coding Agent"] --> reelink["Single Reelink MCP"]
  reelink --> layer0["Layer 0 Raw Video Analysis"]
  reelink --> gateway["Browser Gateway Tools"]
  reelink --> recorder["Recording Lifecycle"]
  gateway --> childMcp["Managed Playwright MCP Child"]
  recorder --> pwLib["Playwright Library"]
  childMcp --> chromium["Shared Chromium via CDP"]
  pwLib --> chromium
  recorder --> reactGrab["Visible React-Grab Workflow"]
  layer0 --> package["Recording Package"]
  reactGrab --> package
  chromium --> package
  package --> retrieval["Frame DOM Component Query Eval"]
  retrieval --> agent
```

## Phase 1: Repair Authority And Status Without Breaking OpenSpec

Files:

- `completion-auditreview.md`
- `REELINK_COMPLETION_AUDIT.md`
- `openspec/changes/build-reelink-mcp/tasks.md`
- `openspec/changes/build-reelink-mcp/design.md`

Work:

- Update authority wording to transcript-first.
- Do not replace OpenSpec checkbox mechanics. The current OpenSpec skills parse checkboxes for progress and verification.
- Keep task checkboxes as the machine-readable source.
- Add or update a status table above the checklist for richer states such as `implemented`, `needs real-app proof`, `docs drift`, `verified`, or `blocked`.
- Use annotations under checkbox items when richer detail is needed.
- Uncheck only items that are materially false as completion claims, and preserve OpenSpec-compatible `- [ ]` / `- [x]` syntax.

Acceptance:

- `tasks.md` remains compatible with OpenSpec tooling.
- Status table and checkbox state no longer contradict each other.
- No file claims the old completion plan outranks the transcript.

## Phase 2: Reconcile OpenSpec Specs And Public Claims

Files:

- `openspec/changes/build-reelink-mcp/proposal.md`
- `openspec/changes/build-reelink-mcp/design.md`
- all specs under `openspec/changes/build-reelink-mcp/specs/`
- all matching specs under `openspec/specs/`

Work:

- Normalize production install to `bunx -y reelink init`, `bunx -y reelink doctor`, and `bunx -y reelink mcp`, with `bun install -g reelink` as the repeated-use option.
- Clearly scope `reelink-mcp`, source-path `bun run .../src/cli.ts mcp`, and `bun link` as local development/testing paths.
- Resolve active-spec versus change-spec sync state.
- Encode one query story: deterministic-first, optional AI SDK fallback only when explicitly enabled and configured, or no fallback if that decision is changed.
- Keep no-rrweb and no-agentation-v0.1 decisions unless the transcript-derived plan is explicitly changed.

Acceptance:

- Public claims match what final verification will prove.

## Phase 3: Lock Findings And Work Items Compatibility Policy

Files:

- `reelink/src/mcp/tools/analysis.ts`
- `reelink/src/analysis/pipeline.ts`
- `reelink/src/schemas/layer0.ts`
- `reelink/src/recordings/store.ts`

- `reelink/tests/analyze-orchestration.test.ts`
- `reelink/tests/mcp-tool-surface.test.ts`
- `reelink/tests/recordings-store.test.ts`

Decision required before implementation:

- Option A: public response is `findings` only; `work_items` remains stored/internal and legacy-readable.
- Option B: public response is `findings + work_items` during a documented compatibility window, with explicit removal criteria.

Recommended default:

- Use Option B for the near-term release to avoid breaking existing recordings/tests, but make `findings` the canonical public contract in docs/specs.
- Define `work_items` as detail/compatibility output, not the headline contract.
- Preserve legacy recording readability in `RecordingStore` regardless of public response choice.

## Phase 6: Browser Gateway And Recording Package Tightening

# Reelink Master Completion Plan V2

## Scope And Authority

This plan supersedes the outdated `/Users/harsha/.cursor/plans/reelink_completion_fb9c0886.plan.md`. Ignore that old plan for execution. Use it only as historical archaeology if a specific claim needs tracing.

Authority order:

1. `transcript-combined.md`, with later user corrections overriding earlier generated architecture.
2. Current `reelink/` implementation as status evidence, not product intent.
3. Reconciled OpenSpec artifacts.
4. `completion-auditreview.md` as a useful transcript-first audit input.
5. `REELINK_COMPLETION_AUDIT.md` only as stale audit history where still accurate.

## Proof Levels

Use these proof levels consistently when updating status:

- `implemented`
- `unit-tested`
- `smoke-verified`
- `real-app-verified`
- `client-verified`
- `complete`

A phase cannot be marked complete until it reaches the proof level required by its acceptance criteria.

## Existing Code Rule

Prefer proving and tightening existing behavior over rewriting it.

If current code already materially satisfies the transcript-defined end state, keep it and verify it. Rewrite only where:

- the transcript-defined requirement is unmet,
- the public contract is inconsistent,
- the behavior is misleading,
- or the implementation is untrustworthy.

This prevents churn and preserves reviewability.

## Final Product Target

Reelink is an open-source video/state primitive for coding agents.

Layer 0 imports existing video and returns structured findings. Raw video to Qwen/OpenRouter is primary where supported; frame extraction is for cached retrieval or fallback providers, not the product thesis.

Layer 1 records browser sessions into timestamp-aligned state packages: video, frames, DOM/runtime state, network, console, trace, react-grab annotation events, React/component/source evidence, manifest, and explicit missing-stream statuses.

Layer 2 records the agent’s own browser work through `reelink_run`, preserves evidence on partial failure, returns `success`, recent observations, and durable eval evidence.

## Product Completion Definition

Reelink is only complete when all three layers are real, user-facing, and verified:

Layer 0 complete means imported videos can be analyzed through the public MCP and CLI surfaces, returning the final public schema, with persisted artifacts and deterministic retrieval over those artifacts.

Layer 1 complete means browser-driven recordings create timestamp-aligned state packages with video, trace, DOM/runtime, network, console, React/source evidence, and those artifacts are queryable through the public MCP surface.

Layer 2 complete means `reelink_run` is a stable public tool that records the agent’s browser task, preserves partial-failure evidence, returns real observation state, and writes durable eval evidence.

No layer counts as complete based on docs, partial code, or fixture-only tests alone.

## Target Architecture

```mermaid
flowchart TD
  agent["Coding Agent"] --> reelink["Single Reelink MCP"]
  reelink --> layer0["Layer 0 Raw Video Analysis"]
  reelink --> gateway["Browser Gateway Tools"]
  reelink --> recorder["Recording Lifecycle"]
  gateway --> childMcp["Managed Playwright MCP Child"]
  recorder --> pwLib["Playwright Library"]
  childMcp --> chromium["Shared Chromium via CDP"]
  pwLib --> chromium
  recorder --> reactGrab["Visible React-Grab Workflow"]
  layer0 --> packageNode["Recording Package"]
  reactGrab --> packageNode
  chromium --> packageNode
  packageNode --> retrieval["Frame DOM Component Query Eval"]
  retrieval --> agent
```

One user-visible MCP is registered: `reelink`. Internally, Reelink may manage Playwright MCP as a child process attached to the same Chromium via CDP. Users should not have to register Playwright MCP separately.

Playwright library owns recording, trace, video, and `addInitScript` ordering. React-grab is default, visible, and product-critical. AI SDK is the model abstraction layer.

## Public API Finalization

This plan must end with a final, stable public contract for all externally visible tools and package behaviors.

Required final decisions:

- Whether `reelink_analyze` returns `findings` only, or `findings` plus `work_items` during a compatibility window.
- The final public response shape for `reelink_get_finding`.
- The final public response shape for `reelink_get_frame`.
- The final public response shape for `reelink_get_dom`.
- The final public response shape for `reelink_get_components`.
- The final public response shape for `reelink_query`.
- The final public response shape for `reelink_run`.
- Legacy recording compatibility rules for old findings-only and work-items-only analysis files.
- Manifest schema stability and migration behavior.

These are product API commitments, not cleanup tasks.

## Phase 1: Authority And Status Reset

Files:

- `completion-auditreview.md`
- `REELINK_COMPLETION_AUDIT.md`
- `openspec/changes/build-reelink-mcp/tasks.md`
- `openspec/changes/build-reelink-mcp/design.md`

Work:

- Update authority wording to transcript-first.
- Do not replace OpenSpec checkbox mechanics. The current OpenSpec skills parse checkboxes for progress and verification.
- Keep task checkboxes as the machine-readable source.

- Use annotations under checkbox items when richer detail is needed.
- Uncheck only items that are materially false as completion claims, and preserve OpenSpec-compatible `- [ ]` / `- [x]` syntax.

Acceptance:

- `tasks.md` remains compatible with OpenSpec tooling.

- No file claims the old completion plan outranks the transcript.

## Phase 2: OpenSpec Reconciliation

## Phase 5: Layer 1 Completion

 ## Context
 
 Reelink is video-as-state for AI browser agents. It turns screen recordings and agent browser sessions into timestamp-aligned state packages: video, findings, DOM timeline, React component/source map, network/console, trace, and eval evidence.
 
 The wedge is still simple: arbitrary video path to structured findings. The thesis is broader: browser/coding agents need a persistent temporal observation primitive. Screenshot/eval/DOM loops force agents to repeatedly request fresh snapshots, spend tokens on state that immediately goes stale, and miss temporal causality. Playwright MCP and similar tools are valuable, and newer CLI/scoped approaches reduce observation cost, but they still operate over discrete snapshots. Video plus DOM/runtime timelines lets agents inspect motion, causality, and state at any timestamp.
+
+## Authority and Completion Definition
+
+Product intent comes from the latest settled user corrections in `transcript-combined.md`; this design and the task tracker encode that intent but do not outrank it. Current code, tests, smoke artifacts, and docs are evidence of implementation status only.
+
+Reelink is complete only when all three layers are real, user-facing, and verified:
+
+- **Layer 0 complete** means imported videos can be analyzed through public MCP and CLI surfaces, returning the final public schema with persisted artifacts and deterministic retrieval over those artifacts.
+- **Layer 1 complete** means browser-driven recordings create timestamp-aligned state packages with video, trace, DOM/runtime, network, console, React/source evidence, and public MCP retrieval over those artifacts.
+- **Layer 2 complete** means `reelink_run` is a stable public tool that records the agent's browser task, preserves partial-failure evidence, returns real observation state, and writes durable eval evidence.
+
+No layer counts as complete based on docs, partial code, or fixture-only tests alone. Use proof levels consistently when updating status: `implemented`, `unit-tested`, `smoke-verified`, `real-app-verified`, `client-verified`, and `complete`.

*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: /Users/harsha/Developer/hackathon/openspec/changes/rename-reelink-to-reck/design.md
+## Context
+
+The product is being renamed from Reelink to Reck before final implementation and public release. This is a cross-cutting public identity change: package metadata, CLI binaries, MCP server name, tool prefixes, environment variables, recording directories, docs, OpenSpec requirements, tests, scripts, and setup snippets all expose the old name today.
+
+The rename should happen before the remaining completion work locks API contracts and verification artifacts. The confirmed external targets are npm package `@anslai/reck`, GitHub repository `anslhq/reck`, primary domain `tryreck.dev`, and defensive domain `tryreck.app`.
+
+## Goals / Non-Goals
+
+**Goals:**
+
+- Rename public product identity from Reelink to Reck.
+- Rename repo package folder from `reelink/` to `reck/`.
+- Rename public binaries to `reck` and `reck-mcp`.
+- Rename public MCP tools from `reelink_*` to `reck_*`.
+- Rename public environment variables from `REELINK_*` to `RECK_*`.
+- Rename recording storage from `.reelink/` to `.reck/`.
+- Update docs, OpenSpec, package metadata, scripts, tests, and setup snippets coherently.
+- Define intentional compatibility for existing `.reelink` recordings and any old env/tool names retained during transition.
+
+**Non-Goals:**
+
+- Do not change the product architecture or layer semantics as part of the rename.
+- Do not use the rename to rewrite working Layer 0/1/2 implementation beyond required name changes.
+- Do not preserve old `reelink_*` public names indefinitely unless explicitly documented as a temporary compatibility window.
+- Do not claim `anslai/reck` as the GitHub target; the confirmed repo target is `anslhq/reck`.
+
+## Decisions
+
+### Decision 1: Rename public surfaces cleanly before final completion work
+
+All public launch surfaces should use Reck naming before API, docs, and release verification are finalized. This includes `@anslai/reck`, `reck`, `reck-mcp`, `reck_*`, `RECK_*`, `.reck/`, `tryreck.dev`, and `anslhq/reck`.
+
+Rationale: changing these after final verification would require re-running client setup, package/distribution checks, docs verification, and runbooks.
+
+### Decision 2: Treat old names as compatibility, not identity
+
+Old names may be accepted only where needed for migration or local continuity, such as reading existing `.reelink/` recordings or honoring old `REELINK_*` environment variables during a documented transition.
+
+Rationale: accidental dual identity creates confusing docs and unstable API expectations. Any dual behavior needs tests and a removal condition.
+
+### Decision 3: Keep OpenSpec checkbox workflow intact
+
+The rename change should create explicit tasks for each rename surface while preserving OpenSpec checkbox syntax. Existing completion change tasks should not be rewritten into another workflow.
+
+Rationale: OpenSpec apply/verify skills parse checkbox state.
+
+### Decision 4: Rename OpenSpec requirements in phases
+
+The rename change introduces a new `reck-product-identity` capability and modifies the six existing capabilities by updating their public names and compatibility behavior. Active specs should only be synced after implementation is verified or during archive/sync according to the repo’s OpenSpec workflow.
+
+Rationale: the rename itself is a product contract change and needs spec coverage before code changes.
+
+## Risks / Trade-offs
+
+- **Risk:** broad search/replace may rename historical context, old audit text, or compatibility references incorrectly. → **Mitigation:** rename public surfaces deliberately and preserve historical references where they explain migration.
+- **Risk:** existing recordings under `.reelink/` become unreadable. → **Mitigation:** keep legacy lookup/migration behavior and tests.
+- **Risk:** old `reelink_*` tools break local clients abruptly. → **Mitigation:** decide whether to provide temporary aliases; if aliases exist, document and test them with removal criteria.
+- **Risk:** package rename changes distribution behavior. → **Mitigation:** verify linked local, global install, package-run, and `tools/list` paths after implementation.
+- **Risk:** docs mention both names inconsistently. → **Mitigation:** require docs verification from scratch before final status sync.
+
+## Migration Plan
+
+1. Update specs and tasks for the rename before implementation.
+2. Rename package folder and package metadata.
+3. Rename binaries and MCP server setup snippets.
+4. Rename public MCP tool names and tests.
+5. Rename env vars and decide temporary old-env fallback behavior.
+6. Rename storage root to `.reck/` and add legacy `.reelink/` compatibility or migration.
+7. Update docs, OpenSpec references, setup prompts, runbooks, and release metadata.
+8. Run full distribution/client/recording verification before syncing status.
+
+## Open Questions
+
+- Whether `reelink_*` MCP tools should remain as temporary aliases for local pre-release clients, or be removed cleanly before public release.
+- Whether `REELINK_*` environment variables should warn and map to `RECK_*` for one compatibility window, or be hard-removed before release.
+- Whether an explicit migration command is needed for `.reelink/` to `.reck/`, or legacy read-only lookup is enough.
*** End Patch
PATCH

<timestamp>Thursday, Apr 30, 2026, 11:24 PM (UTC+10)</timestamp>
<user_query>
Findings
OpenSpec is internally inconsistent about the rename, so it cannot currently serve as a clean source of truth for the product transition.
openspec/changes/build-reelink-mcp/design.md says the public identity changes to Reck, including package, binaries, MCP prefix, env vars, storage root, and package folder, but the same file still uses old names for the core API, install flow, folder examples, and product narrative. proposal.md has the same problem: it declares a breaking rename to Reck while still specifying bunx -y reelink, reelink_* tools, .reelink/, and “Reelink” as the active product in many normative statements. video-finding-analysis/spec.md is even more conflicted because it adds a “Reck” scenario while leaving the canonical scenarios and reconciliation guardrails centered on reelink_*.
This is the highest-severity issue because PRODUCT.md and the new reck-product-identity spec imply a decisive rename, while the rest of OpenSpec still describes a hybrid state with no explicit compatibility window structure. That means implementers can truthfully “follow the spec” and still produce incompatible outcomes.

tasks.md still overclaims implementation/verification relative to the product bar defined in PRODUCT.md and reinforced by GAPLIST.md.
The status table in openspec/changes/build-reelink-mcp/tasks.md says Layer 1, Layer 2, provider strategy, package semantics, and workflow proof are “implemented” with remediation remaining, and many downstream tasks in P2/P3 are written as verified by tests/smokes. But PRODUCT.md explicitly says Layer 1 is not complete until runtime is verified on a real supported app, and Layer 2 is not complete until the public workflow is proven end-to-end. GAPLIST.md carries the same standard.
So even though tasks.md is better than older versions, it still reads too close to “feature done, proof polishing left” instead of “major product claims still pending real-app/client-grade verification.” That mismatch matters because OpenSpec task status is exactly where false completion pressure creeps back in.

OpenSpec still encodes hackathon-era framing that conflicts with the current product definition.
design.md still includes goals like “Provide a hackathon plan that can demo video-to-finding quickly” and long demo-specific sections around founder portfolio bugs. That historical context is useful, but in the current state it is mixed into normative design text rather than clearly separated as historical execution context. PRODUCT.md defines a production-grade open-source dev tool, and your current direction is explicitly “well past the hackathon.”
As written, OpenSpec still partly frames the product around the demo and submission path, which weakens alignment with both PRODUCT.md and GAPLIST.md.

The product contract around findings vs work_items is improved but still not fully resolved across OpenSpec.
video-finding-analysis/spec.md correctly says findings remains the public authority and any public work_items field must be treated as compatibility/detail output. But design.md still uses reelink_analyze examples that normalize older shapes, and tasks.md still includes old success criteria phrased around work_items in places.
This is a medium-high issue because PRODUCT.md and GAPLIST.md both identify this as a core contract decision, and OpenSpec still reads like it wants both clarity and ambiguity at the same time.

OpenSpec’s doc-path and package-path expectations are inconsistent with the rename plan.
video-finding-analysis/spec.md still says onboarding docs must exist under reelink/docs/, while reck-product-identity/spec.md says the package folder should become reck/. design.md also says the root package folder becomes reck/, but many referenced paths remain under reelink/ and many examples still use .reelink/.
That creates a practical migration ambiguity: is the rename intended to be purely public-facing first, or does OpenSpec require an immediate filesystem/package rename too? Right now it implies both, but not cleanly.

The Codex/client onboarding requirements are still tied to old naming and commands, so they conflict with the new identity spec.
video-finding-analysis/spec.md still requires writing [mcp_servers.reelink], verifying reelink is running, and calling reelink_analyze, while the new identity spec requires reck_* as the public MCP surface and reck / reck-mcp binaries.
This is a concrete example of why the rename is not yet spec-coherent: even the “canonical client” flow can’t be implemented exactly as written once the rename is enforced.

PRODUCT.md and GAPLIST.md are now out of sync with the current OpenSpec rename decision.
This is not an OpenSpec bug alone, but it is a real alignment problem. Both files still present Reelink as the current product identity, while OpenSpec now says the final public identity is Reck. Since you asked for review against those files, the honest answer is that they are no longer fully aligned.
If Reck is the settled name, then PRODUCT.md and GAPLIST.md need a rename-aware revision. If Reck is still provisional, OpenSpec is prematurely acting as though the rename is locked.
</user_query>

So even though tasks.md is better than older versions, it still reads too close to “feature done, proof polishing left” instead of “major product claims still pending real-app/client-grade verification.” That mismatch matters because OpenSpec task status is exactly where false completion pressure creeps back in.

OpenSpec still encodes hackathon-era framing that conflicts with the current product definition.
design.md still includes goals like “Provide a hackathon plan that can demo video-to-finding quickly” and long demo-specific sections around founder portfolio bugs. That historical context is useful, but in the current state it is mixed into normative design text rather than clearly separated as historical execution context. PRODUCT.md defines a production-grade open-source dev tool, and your current direction is explicitly “well past the hackathon.”
As written, OpenSpec still partly frames the product around the demo and submission path, which weakens alignment with both PRODUCT.md and GAPLIST.md.

The product contract around findings vs work_items is improved but still not fully resolved across OpenSpec.
video-finding-analysis/spec.md correctly says findings remains the public authority and any public work_items field must be treated as compatibility/detail output. But design.md still uses reelink_analyze examples that normalize older shapes, and tasks.md still includes old success criteria phrased around work_items in places.
This is a medium-high issue because PRODUCT.md and GAPLIST.md both identify this as a core contract decision, and OpenSpec still reads like it wants both clarity and ambiguity at the same time.

OpenSpec’s doc-path and package-path expectations are inconsistent with the rename plan.
video-finding-analysis/spec.md still says onboarding docs must exist under reelink/docs/, while reck-product-identity/spec.md says the package folder should become reck/. design.md also says the root package folder becomes reck/, but many referenced paths remain under reelink/ and many examples still use .reelink/.
That creates a practical migration ambiguity: is the rename intended to be purely public-facing first, or does OpenSpec require an immediate filesystem/package rename too? Right now it implies both, but not cleanly.

The Codex/client onboarding requirements are still tied to old naming and commands, so they conflict with the new identity spec.
video-finding-analysis/spec.md still requires writing [mcp_servers.reelink], verifying reelink is running, and calling reelink_analyze, while the new identity spec requires reck_* as the public MCP surface and reck / reck-mcp binaries.
This is a concrete example of why the rename is not yet spec-coherent: even the “canonical client” flow can’t be implemented exactly as written once the rename is enforced.

PRODUCT.md and GAPLIST.md are now out of sync with the current OpenSpec rename decision.
This is not an OpenSpec bug alone, but it is a real alignment problem. Both files still present Reelink as the current product identity, while OpenSpec now says the final public identity is Reck. Since you asked for review against those files, the honest answer is that they are no longer fully aligned.
If Reck is the settled name, then PRODUCT.md and GAPLIST.md need a rename-aware revision. If Reck is still provisional, OpenSpec is prematurely acting as though the rename is locked.
